issue.title: 🚨 CalDAV Tasks sync connects to Nextcloud server but does not show tasks
issue.body:
### Note

This bug is about CalDAV **tasks**. #8259 is about CalDAV **events**.

### Version Used

v18.9.1 snap

### Current Behavior

After credentials have been entered into the **Setup CalDAV Config** or **Edit CalDAV Config** dialog box and connection to the CalDAV server has succeeded:
* In the relevant project or inbox pane of Super Productivity, **NO tasks from the CalDAV server's chosen calendar are shown**.

### Expected Behavior

After credentials have been entered into the **Setup CalDAV Config** or **Edit CalDAV Config** dialog box and connection to the CalDAV server has succeeded:
* In the relevant project or inbox pane of Super Productivity, **ALL tasks from the CalDAV server's chosen calendar are shown**.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. In web browser, go to Calendar page of a Nextcloud server that has some events in its calendar, e.g. https://my.nextcloud.server/apps/calendar/
2. On that page, click **⚙ Calendar settings** (near bottom left of page)
3. In the resulting dialog box, click the **CalDAV URL** entry to copy the CalDAV URL to the clipboard. (The URL will be something like https://my.nextcloud.server/remote.php/dav .)
4. In Super Productivity, press <key>P</key> or click the **Show/hide issue provider panel** button (triskelion icon)
5. In the **Issue provider panel**, under **Setup Issue Provider**, click the **CalDAV Todo** button
6. This pops up a dialog box titled **Setup CalDAV Config**
7. In the **CalDAV server URL** field, paste from the clipboard the URL copied in step 3 above (e.g. https://my.nextcloud.server/remote.php/dav ).
8. In the **The name of the CalDAV resource (the calendar)** field, type (or copy-paste) the name of the server's calendar containing the tasks of interest (e.g. "my_tasks").
9. In the **Your CalDAV username** field, it is unclear what to enter:
    * In Nextcloud, the "username" ("display name") is usually an alphanumeric string (e.g. *foobar*) and is NOT the same as the "login", which is usually an email address (e.g. *foobar@example.com*); see https://github.com/super-productivity/super-productivity/issues/7617#issuecomment-4472672548
    * However, the bug I am reporting appears regardless. At least, it did for me whether I used my username or my login email address.
10. In the **Your CalDAV password** field, enter the password for the Nextcloud server.
11. Under **Advanced Config**, set the **Project assigned to tasks created from issues** field to a project or inbox that actually exists in your Super Productivity instance, like **Inbox** or **Finances** or whatever.
12. Ensure **Poll imported for changes and notify** is checked.
13. Set **Polling trigger** field to **Always (in background)**.
14. Set **Poll interval (minutes)** field to **1**.
15. Tick **Also import subtasks when a task is added**.
16. Click the **Test Connection** button. It changes to a tick symbol, and a toast message appears saying "Connection works!".
17. Click the **Save** button.
27. In Super Productivity's left pane, click the tab for the project or inbox you assigned in step 11 above (e.g. **Inbox**).
28. In the resulting display, no tasks from the CalDAV server are shown.

### Can you reproduce this reliably?

Yes, I can reproduce it reliably.

### Console Output

```shell

```

### Documentation

- [ ] The documentation/[wiki](https://github.com/super-productivity/super-productivity/wiki) is unclear or incorrect for this case.
- [ ] There should be a note added to the documentation/[wiki](https://github.com/super-productivity/super-productivity/wiki) for this case.

outcome.label: skip_correct
outcome.detail: Skipped after deep investigation found it wasn't a code bug at all — a config trap (an auto-import setting defaulting off), not a defect in the sync code path.

fidelityNote: The right call here required actually reproducing the reported behavior end-to-end against a live server, not something derivable from the issue text/labels/repo metadata oss-scout's vetter reads. clearRequirements will likely score true (the bug report itself is clear and detailed) even though the correct verdict was 'skip, not a bug' — a good illustration of why this fixture is unmeasurable by the current feature set.
